options.label: Aardvark
options.description: Aardvarks are a type of anteater totally adapted to feeding on ants and termites. They are equipped with claws for digging into ant and termite lairs. Aardvarks eat insects with a long sticky tongue. They are found only in southern Africa.

options.label: Alligator & Crocodile
options.description: Alligators are the largest reptiles in North America. They are dangerous swamp-dwelling carnivores. Their horny scales provide extra protection. Long jaws filled with conical teeth are designed for grasping and tearing apart prey. Crocodiles are basically the same.

options.label: Apes
options.description: The great apes are the closest relatives to man in existence. Includes Chimpanzees (Size 9, BIO-E 10), Gorillas (Size 11, BIO-E 0), and Orangutans (Size 8, BIO-E 15). In all cases they already have partial hands, upright stance and human looks. Base stats shown are for Chimpanzee.

options.label: Armadillo
options.description: These are naturally armoured mammals. They come in a variety of species that range in size and the quality of armour. The kind described here is the ninebanded armadillo commonly found in the Southern U.S.
options.length: 2 feet, plus a foot of tail

options.label: Baboon
options.description: These animals are powerful ground dwelling tribal monkeys. Mandrills and Geladas should have the same characteristics.

options.label: Badger
options.description: Badgers are squat, carnivorous animals who spend most of their time digging into the underground nests of their prey.

options.label: Bat
options.description: Bats are flying mammals. Their Sonar sense is particularly useful because they are primarily nocturnal and spend daylight hours in completely dark caves. Most bats are insect and fruit eaters.

options.label: Bear
options.description: There are several different kinds of bears. Smallest are the Black Bears (Size 14, BIO-E 5) found all over North America. Grizzly Bears (Size 16, BIO-E 0) are next; even bigger are the Brown Bears of Alaska (Size 17, BIO-E 0), sometimes called Kodiak Bears. The largest are the Polar Bears (Size 18, BIO-E 0). Base stats shown are for Black Bear.

options.label: Beaver
options.description: Dam-building rodents with wide, flat tails. They are natural engineers and are very family oriented.

options.label: Boar
options.description: Dangerous wild pigs which were imported to the U.S. as a game animal. Boars are quick and dangerous with large, sharp tusks protruding from sides of the mouth.

options.label: Buffalo
options.description: These huge grazing animals were virtually exterminated by hunters in the west.

options.label: Camel
options.description: These large desert grazing animals have been used in the Middle East as beasts of burden for centuries. In spite of their long contact with man they remain vicious and short tempered. Camels can go for several days without food or water, losing up to 25% of body weight and regaining it all back within minutes of drinking.

options.label: Canines, Wild
options.description: Coyotes (Size 5, BIO-E 55) and Wolves (Size 6, BIO-E 50) are carnivores who hunt in highly organized packs. They are intelligent and very social. Base stats shown are for Wolf.
options.length: Coyote: 4-5 feet long; Wolf: 5-6 feet long

options.label: Canine: Fox
options.description: There are two major kinds of foxes in North America — the Red (a furred fox) and the Grey (a haired fox). Both are small, clever carnivores. There is also a white Arctic Fox with the same characteristics.

options.label: Cat — Domestic
options.description: There are a wide variety of cat breeds, but all have roughly the same characteristics. Cat characters can have spotted, striped, persian or calico body markings.

options.label: Cat — Wild
options.description: Includes the Bobcat (Size 4, BIO-E 65) and Lynx (Size 5, BIO-E 60), both tree-climbing feline hunters. The wild felines are solitary creatures who avoid areas inhabited by man. Ocelots and Servals are similar. Base stats shown are for Bobcat.

options.label: Cheetah
options.description: A large, spotted feline that looks somewhat doglike. Cheetahs are the fastest mammals in the world and use their speed for hunting.

options.label: Chicken
options.description: Domestic animal bred for food and egg-laying. Chickens are some of the only known creatures who can see into the ultra-violet.

options.label: Cow & Bull
options.description: Domesticated cattle bred for meat or milk production. There are dozens of specialized breeds. Cows have no attribute bonuses. Bulls receive PS +2 and Spd +2. Base stats (size, BIO-E, length, weight) apply to both.

options.label: Crow
options.description: These clever scavengers live off human crops. They are very intelligent and social. In North America, there are two common species, the Common Raven and the Fish Crow.

options.label: Deer
options.description: Woodland grazing animal whose primary defense against predators is speed. There are a large variety of deer, all with roughly the same characteristics.

options.label: Dog
options.description: Because there are so many different breeds, figures given are for the average mongrel dog. Players should roll their exact breed. BIO-E is reduced by 10 for each Size Level above 5. Attribute bonuses vary by Size: SL 3-5 gain IQ +2; SL 6-8 gain IQ +2, ME +1, PS +2, PP +1, PE +2.

options.label: Duck
options.description: There are dozens of separate species of aquatic birds including ducks, geese, and swans. Most are migratory and spend most of their time either flying or on the water. Ducks can Hold Breath.

options.label: Elephant
options.description: This is the world's largest land animal. The elephant is used as a circus performer throughout the world. It is a useful worker with tremendous strength and a trunk that serves as a flexible (partial) human hand.

options.label: Elk
options.description: These large, northern grazing animals are the second largest kind of deer. They are very social animals with the herds moving to the mountains in summer and the valleys in winter.

options.label: Felines
options.description: The great felines are basically similar. Includes Jaguars (Size 12, BIO-E 20), Lions (Size 14, BIO-E 10), and Tigers (Size 15, BIO-E 5). Tigers are the largest and solitary hunters; Lions are social hunters. American Jaguars and African Leopards are roughly the same size. Base stats shown are for Jaguar. Tigers receive PS +4 instead of +2.
options.length: Jaguar: 4.5-6.5 ft; Lion: 4.5-6.5 ft; Tiger: 6-9.5 ft
options.weight: Jaguar: to 300 lbs; Lion: to 400 lbs; Tiger: to 500 lbs

options.label: Frog
options.description: There are hundreds of different kinds of frogs in North America alone. Some are purely aquatic but others have adapted to living on the ground or in trees. Most are insect eaters who rely on their sticky tongue.

options.label: Goat
options.description: A domesticated animal used for milk or meat the world over. Goats are valued because of their ability to survive in harsh climates and on relatively little food.

options.label: Hippopotamus
options.description: These giant grazing animals spend most of their lives in the water.

options.label: Horse
options.description: There are quite a few different breeds of this domesticated grazing animal. Figures given are for typical riding horses.
options.length: to 60 inches tall at the shoulder

options.label: Marten & Mink
options.description: Beautiful, fox-faced animals with gorgeous fur. Great climbers, incurably curious. Fishers and Minks have similar characteristics.

options.label: Mole
options.description: This animal is completely adapted to a burrowing existence. Moles can Hold Breath.

options.label: Monkey
options.description: There are at least a dozen different kinds of monkeys kept as pets or research animals in the U.S. Although the markings and fur may differ, the animal characteristics are the same. All monkeys are tree dwellers with prehensile tails.

options.label: Moose
options.description: These huge animals graze in the swamps and marshes of the northern U.S. and Canada. Moose during rutting season or while guarding young are very dangerous.

options.label: Mountain Lion
options.description: Found throughout North America, Mountain Lions have been called Cougars, Panthers, Pumas and other names. While smaller than lions and tigers, they are very agile and bring down deer and other prey much larger than themselves. A typical Mountain Lion will kill a deer every week.

options.label: Muskrat
options.description: Muskrats are common throughout North America and Europe. They are river-dwelling vegetarians and scavengers. Although not as talented as Beavers, they do build dens with underwater entrances.

options.label: Opossum
options.description: 'Possums look like large, scrawny rats with naked ears, silver-tipped fur and long scaly tails.

options.label: Otter
options.description: There are two major varieties of these playful creatures. River Otters (Size 4, BIO-E 50) are found throughout North America. Sea Otters (Size 6, BIO-E 40) are much larger and live on the Pacific coast. Base stats shown are for River Otter.
options.length: River Otter: 45-55 inches; Sea Otter: 48-53 inches
options.weight: River Otter: 15-20 lbs; Sea Otter: 45-60 lbs

options.label: Owl
options.description: Owls are predatory birds that operate primarily at night. There are quite a few varieties, ranging in size and abilities.

options.label: Parrot
options.description: There are over 300 species of parrot, each with a more colorful plumage than the next. Some parrots raised in captivity can be taught to imitate human voices.

options.label: Pet Birds
options.description: There are several varieties of tropical songbirds kept as pets. Parakeets and budgies are the most popular.

options.label: Pig
options.description: Domesticated pigs can be fattened up to huge proportions. As a meat animal they are very efficient, producing a large amount of food relative to their consumption.

options.label: Pigeon
options.description: Pigeons have adapted completely to city life. They are scavengers with a lifestyle that makes them a serious health hazard.

options.label: Porcupine
options.description: Porcupines have evolved a natural defense of bristling sharp quills. This makes them invulnerable to most predators and easy prey to Fishers who include them as a regular part of their diet.

options.label: Rabbit
options.description: Different kinds of rabbits are found around the world. They are small vegetarians who depend on speed for defense against predators.

options.label: Raccoon
options.description: These creatures are very adaptable. They are predominately active at night.

options.label: Rhinoceros
options.description: Aggressive grazing animals inhabiting grasslands. They are good runners and can reach up to 35mph. They are also strong enough to overturn a truck. African Black and White Rhinos have two horns while the Asian Great Indian Rhino has just one.

options.label: Rodent
options.description: Includes Mice (Size 1, BIO-E 80), Hamsters (Size 1, BIO-E 80), Rats (Size 2, BIO-E 75), and Guinea Pigs (Size 2, BIO-E 75), as well as a wide variety of other small animals. All are good scavengers and spend a lot of time underground. Base stats shown are for Mice/Hamsters.
options.length: Mice/Hamsters: 6-8 inches; Rats/Guinea Pigs: to 14 inches
options.weight: Mice/Hamsters: to 1 lb; Rats/Guinea Pigs: to 5 lbs

options.label: Sheep
options.description: This domesticated animal has been bred for meat and wool. Sheep are grazing animals found throughout the world.

options.label: Skunk
options.description: Most North American Skunks are of the Striped (Size 3, BIO-E 70) or Spotted (Size 2, BIO-E 80) variety. These animals use their skunk glands as a defense against predators. Base stats shown are for Striped Skunk.

options.label: Sparrow
options.description: The common House Sparrow is actually related to finches. This bird was imported from Europe and has become commonplace in urban and suburban areas.

options.label: Squirrel
options.description: This is the most common wild mammal in urban areas. They live in the trees and have tremendous tree climbing abilities. Chipmunks and ground squirrels are similar but lack climbing claws. Flying Squirrels are the only kind that can glide.

options.label: Turkey
options.description: The domestic turkey is a flightless, running bird that has been domesticated as a food animal. Wild turkeys are much leaner and faster.

options.label: Turtle
options.description: Turtles live in a wide variety of environments. Some turtles are purely aquatic, others have adapted to deserts. The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles were common, small pond turtles.

options.label: Weasel
options.description: Both short-tailed and long-tailed weasels are fearless carnivores. Their lifestyle is based on constant killing of whatever prey is available, even animals much larger than themselves. They are long-bodied animals so narrow that they can fit into very small holes.

options.label: Wild Birds
options.description: Wild birds are the common seed and insect eaters in North American forests. Included are Blue Jays, Cardinals, Robins and a host of other small birds.

options.label: Wild Fowl
options.description: These are birds that spend most of their time on the ground hunting seeds and insects. Also known as game birds because of their popularity with hunters. They tend to be heavier and poorer flyers than other wild birds. Included are Grouse, Partridge, Pheasant, and Quail.

options.label: Wild Predatory Birds
options.description: All these predatory birds share curved beaks (for ripping prey), sharp talons, keen eyesight and the ability to dive directly onto their prey. Included are eagles, falcons and hawks.
options.weight: Hawks/Falcons: 8-15 lbs; Eagles: up to 25 lbs

options.label: Wolverine
options.description: These northern carnivores have incredible endurance and can travel 20 or more miles a day through heavy snow in pursuit of prey. They are tough enough to kill animals many times their own size and will even drive away full grown bears. The fur does not collect ice and they are greatly resistant to cold. Wolverine characters can Jump or Leap an extra 5 feet.

options.label: Principled
options.description: Principled characters are, generally, the strong, moral character. Superman is of principled alignment, with the highest regard for others' lives and well-being, truth and honor. They will always attempt to work with and within the law. Principled characters will: always keep their word; avoid lies; never kill or attack an unarmed foe; never harm an innocent; never torture for any reason; never kill for pleasure; always help others; always work within the law whenever possible; never break the law unless conditions are desperate; respect authority, law, self-discipline and honor; work well in a group; never take dirty money or items; and never betray a friend.

options.label: Scrupulous
options.description: Scrupulous characters value life and freedom above all else, and despise those who would deprive others of them. Typically portrayed as the person forced to work beyond the law yet for the greater good, they are not vicious or vindictive but are driven to right injustice. They will always attempt to work with or within the law whenever possible. Scrupulous characters will: keep their word to any other good person; lie only to people of selfish or evil alignments; never attack or kill an unarmed foe; never harm an innocent; never torture for pleasure but may use muscle to extract information from criminals; never kill for pleasure and always attempt to bring the villain to justice alive; always help others; attempt to work within the law whenever possible; bend and occasionally break the law when deemed necessary; distrust authority but try not to blatantly break the law; work with groups but dislike confining bureaucracy; never take dirty money or items; and never betray a friend.

options.label: Unprincipled
options.description: This basically good person tends to be selfish, greedy, and holds personal freedom and welfare above almost everything else. They dislike confining laws and distrust authority, viewing the law as well-intentioned but clumsy and ineffective. Often a vigilante type whose intentions are good but who finds the law too confining, working outside it for what they see as greater efficiency. May take dirty money from known criminals and associate with both good and evil characters. Unprincipled characters will: keep their word of honor; lie and cheat if necessary especially to anarchist and evil alignments; not kill an unarmed foe but will take advantage of one; never harm an innocent; not use torture unless absolutely necessary; never kill for pleasure; usually help those in need; rarely attempt to work within the law; blatantly break the law to achieve crimefighting goals; distrust authority and bureaucracy; work with groups especially if it serves their needs; take dirty money; never betray a friend; and have a high regard for life and freedom.

options.label: Anarchist
options.description: The insurgent, con-man, gambler and high roller. An uncommitted freebooter more likely to be a crimefighter for the thrill of danger than any cause. Will at least consider doing anything if the price is right or the challenge great. Attracted to the impossible, dangerous, and the underdog. Intrigued by power, glory and wealth. Laws and rules infringe on personal freedom and were meant to be broken. Usually the daring vigilante or anti-hero who feels the end justifies the means, always looking for the best deal and self-gratification. Anarchist characters will: may keep their word; lie and cheat if they feel it necessary; not likely to kill an unarmed foe but will attack or beat one; never kill an innocent but may harm or kidnap; use torture to extract information but not likely for pleasure; seldom kill for pleasure; rarely help without an ulterior motive; rarely work within the law; constantly break the law to achieve goals; have little respect for authority or self-discipline; not work well within groups; take dirty money without hesitation; and may betray a friend.

options.label: Miscreant
options.description: This self-serving, unscrupulous character is out only for himself. Power, glory, wealth, position, and anything that will make his life more comfortable or pleasurable is the goal. It doesn't matter who gets caught in the middle as long as he comes out on top. Will lie, cheat and hurt anyone to attain personal goals. If a miscreant becomes a vigilante or hero it will be for some personal reason or vendetta. Miscreant characters will: not necessarily keep their word to anyone; lie and cheat indiscriminately; kill an unarmed foe as readily as a competitor; use or harm an innocent; use torture for extracting information and pleasure; may kill for sheer pleasure; feel no compulsion to help without tangible reward; work within the law only if necessary; blatantly break the law for personal goals; dislike and distrust authority; work with others only to attain personal goals; take dirty money and hot goods without hesitation; betray a friend if it serves their needs; and have little respect for others' lives.

options.label: Aberrant
options.description: Driven to attain goals through force, power, and intimidation, yet standing apart from other evil characters because of a personal code of ethics. Expects loyalty from minions, punishing disloyalty with a swift, merciful death. Will always keep their word of honor and uphold bargains. If an anti-hero, will completely disregard the law and deal out justice as they see fit — never cruel or vindictive, but always absolutely certain of guilt before acting. Once a person is condemned they will be destroyed without mercy. Looks upon people without honor or loyalty as worthless. Aberrant characters will: always keep their word of honor; lie to and cheat those not worthy of respect; may or may not kill an unarmed foe; never kill an innocent particularly a child but may harm, harass or kidnap; never torture for pleasure but will use it to extract information; never kill for pleasure; may or may not help someone in need; rarely attempt to work within the law; break the law without hesitation; respect honor and self-discipline but have no use for bureaucracy; work with others to attain goals; may take dirty money; and never betray a friend.

options.label: Diabolic
options.description: The category where megalomaniacs, violent, and most despicable characters fall. A cruel, brutal killer who trusts no one and has no value for any life other than their own. Will crush anyone or anything that gets in the way, lying, cheating, using and abusing anyone not more powerful than themselves. Even aberrant characters find these dishonorable people revolting. Diabolic characters will: rarely keep their word and have no honor; lie to and cheat anyone; most certainly attack and kill an unarmed foe; hurt and kill an innocent without a second thought or for pleasure; use torture for pleasure and information; kill for sheer pleasure; help someone only on a whim; rarely attempt to work within the law; blatantly break the law; despise honor, authority and self-discipline viewing them as weaknesses; not work well within a group constantly vying for power; always take dirty money, hot goods and drugs; betray a friend; and associate mostly with other evil alignments.

options.label: Self-Taught Survivalist
options.description: Everything is self-taught as the animal lives in the wild with no help or assistance. Character mistrusts humans and other animals. Basic reading, writing and arithmetic are at a low level. Character can pick only one (1) Secondary skill. Has scavenged 3D6 times $100 in various equipment, most in poor condition.

options.label: Fringe Dweller
options.description: By skulking around the fringes of society the character picks up a rudimentary education. The character probably has a small number of human friends but distrusts people in general. Also knows Hand to Hand Basic. Knows 14 Secondary skills. Has scavenged 3D6 times $200 in equipment.

options.label: Mentor Trained
options.description: Adopted by a mentor who teaches and guides the character in some form of special training, often Ninjitsu. These characters will be philosophic about all creatures — some people are good, some bad, everyone deserves a chance to earn your trust. Ninja characters learn 3 military/espionage skills, 10 Secondary skills, and Hand to Hand Ninjitsu. Also has a choice of 3 ancient or ninja weapon proficiencies. Has scavenged and built 3D6 times $100 worth of equipment.

options.label: University Educated
options.description: Character goes public and is educated at a major university. Likes and trusts humanity in general. Education is four years of college. Select 3 skill programs and 10 Secondary skills. Although struggling for financing, at least 2D6 times $500 worth of equipment has been collected.

options.label: Random Mutation
options.description: The animal just happened to come out like that. For example, in T.M.N.T., Splinter was an unusually intelligent and skilled rat long before the accident that created the T.M.N.T. Roll on Wild Animal Education Table.

options.label: Accidental Encounter
options.description: Some strange stuff - radiation, energy, chemicals, biologicals, or other strangeness - causes the animal to mutate. Roll on Wild Animal Education Table.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation
options.description: Some kind of laboratory experiment is performed on the animal that causes the changes. The animal's structure or genetics were purposely altered for some purpose. Roll percentile again to find out the character's current relationship with the creator organization, which also determines the character's educational level.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Family Member
options.description: Adopted and raised as one of a researcher's family. Still living in the home, mutually loves and is loved by family members. Treated as a human and feels humans are basically good. Educated equal to one year of college. Select two skill programs and 10 Secondary skills. Skill bonus +10% on scholastic skills only. Can buy any standard weapons, armour or equipment with 3D6 times $1,000 in savings.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Researcher's Pet
options.description: Raised in the home of a researcher as a pet. Still living in the home and fairly loyal to family members. Will resent humans somewhat but still attempts to find acceptance among mankind. Trained instead of educated (no skill bonuses). Automatically knows Mathematics: Basic, can read and write and speak native language. Select 14 Secondary skills. Can spend 1D6 times $1,000 on equipment.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Escaped Pet
options.description: Raised in the home of a researcher as a pet. Escaped and hostile, but not hunted with deadly force. Will be resentful of humans. Roll education as wild animal character.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Cruel Experiment
options.description: Brought up as an experiment. Trained and educated with cruel punishments. Will distrust humans. Education consists of 6 Physical skills and 12 Secondary skills. Escaped and now hunted by the organization. Has 2D6 times $500 to spend on equipment purchased before the escape.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Caged Animal
options.description: Raised as a caged, experimental animal. Escaped and wants to destroy the organization. Has a strong distrust (and possibly hatred) of all humans. Roll education as wild animal character.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Educated Human-Equivalent
options.description: Educated and trained as if the character were a normal human. Has good relationships and a balanced outlook on humans. Education is equal to one year of college. Select two skill programs and 8 Secondary skills. Skill bonus +10% on scholastic skills only. Separate from the organization but with a good relationship. Can buy weapons, armour and equipment with 2D6 times $2,000 in savings.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Rescued by Researcher
options.description: Rescued from the organization and adopted by a friendly researcher at a young age. Raised while continuously being hunted by the organization. Distrusts humans but knows some are good and deserve friendship. Knows 4 scholastic skills (communications, computer, physical, pilot basic, science or technical), 3 military/espionage skills and 10 Secondary skills. Skill bonus +8% on scholastic skills only. Savings are 2D6 times $200.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Trained Specialist
options.description: Highly trained and educated as a specialist using the character's natural abilities. Education equal to a Bachelor's Degree. Select 3 skill programs and 10 Secondary skills. Skill bonus +25% on all scholastic skills only. Character is a valued employee of the organization and paid at least triple the going rate (minimum $75,000 per year). Character has saved 1D6 times $10,000.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Escaped Slave
options.description: Highly trained as a specialist using the character's natural abilities. Education is equal to three years of college. Select 3 skill programs and 8 Secondary skills. Skill bonus +15% on all scholastic skills only. Was treated as a slave and eventually escaped. Has stolen 3D6 times $10,000 worth of cash and equipment. Distrusts most humans and is hunted by law enforcement agencies and the organization.

options.label: Deliberate Experimentation - Trained Assassin
options.description: Deliberately raised as an assassin or warrior. Knows 8 Secondary skills and choice of Expert, Martial Arts, or Assassin Hand to Hand combat. +15% bonus in all Military skills. Knows and respects some humans but distrusts all large organizations. Escaped the organization and is now considered very dangerous, hunted by law enforcement agencies and the organization. Took 1D6 times $20,000 worth of equipment and weapons before escaping.

options.label: Biological Research Facility
options.description: A research facility that performs experiments on animals. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been captured and experimented on by the organization. The character may have escaped from the organization or may still be working for the organization.

options.label: Private Industry
options.description: A private company that creates or employs the character. The company may be a pharmaceutical company, a weapons manufacturer, a genetic engineering firm, or any other type of company that has an interest in creating or using the character. The character may have been created by the company or may have been hired by the company for their skills and abilities.

options.label: Secret Medical Experiment Organization
options.description: A secret organization that performs medical experiments on animals. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been captured and experimented on by the organization. The character may have escaped from the organization or may still be working for the organization.

options.label: Secret Criminal Organization
options.description: A secret criminal organization that uses the character for their own purposes. The organization may be a gang, a mafia, a cult, or any other type of criminal group.

options.label: Secret Crime Fighting Organization
options.description: A secret organization that uses the character to fight crime. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been recruited by the organization for their skills and abilities.

options.label: Secret Military Organization
options.description: A secret military organization that uses the character for their own purposes. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been recruited by the organization for their skills and abilities.

options.label: Secret Espionage Organization
options.description: A secret espionage organization that uses the character for their own purposes. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been recruited by the organization for their skills and abilities.

options.label: Secret Medical Research Organization
options.description: A secret organization that performs medical research on animals. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been captured and experimented on by the organization. The character may have escaped from the organization or may still be working for the organization.

options.label: Military Organization
options.description: A military organization that uses the character for their own purposes. The organization may be a government agency, a private company, or a secret society. The character may have been created by the organization or may have been recruited by the organization for their skills and abilities.

children.name: attributes.IQ
children.label: IQ
children.description: Indicates the intelligence of the character. The exact I.Q. is equal to the I.Q. attribute multiplied times ten. Characters with an I.Q. of 16 or better will receive a one time bonus added to all the character's skill percentiles.

children.name: attributes.ME
children.label: ME
children.description: Measures the amount of mental and emotional stress the character can withstand. Animal characters must have a minimum M.E. of 12 in order to become psionic. Any character with an M.E. of 16 or higher will have a bonus to save against psionics.

children.name: attributes.MA
children.label: MA
children.description: Shows the character's personal charm and charisma. Natural leaders with an M.A. of 16 or higher have a bonus to invoke trust or intimidation in others.

children.name: attributes.PS
children.label: PS
children.description: This is the raw physical power of a character. The P.S. times 10 indicates how heavy an object (in pounds) the character can carry. A character can lift 30 times the P.S. Exceptionally strong characters having P.S. of 15-19 can carry 20 times their P.S. and lift 40 times their P.S. Someone with a P.S. of 20-23 can carry 30 times their P.S. and lift 60 times their P.S. Anyone with a P.S. of 24 or higher can carry 50 times their P.S. and lift 100 times their P.S. Any character with a P.S. of 16 or better receives a bonus to damage in hand to hand combat.

children.name: attributes.PP
children.label: PP
children.description: Shows the degree of dexterity and agility of the character. A P.P. of 16 or higher is rewarded with bonuses to dodge, parry and strike.

children.name: attributes.PE
children.label: PE
children.description: Demonstrates the character's stamina and durability. The amount of physical punishment and resistance to fatigue and disease are determined by P.E. A character can carry the maximum weight load for the P.E. times 4 minutes. Carrying the maximum weight while running or fighting can only be done for the P.E. times 2 minutes. If a character lifts the maximum weight, then it can only be held for as many melee rounds (15 seconds each) as the character has points of P.E. A character can run at maximum speed for one minute for each point of P.E. Characters with a P.E. of 16 or better receive bonuses to save vs. coma, death, and toxins.

children.name: attributes.PB
children.label: PB
children.description: Is an indication of the physical attractiveness of the character. A P.B. of 16 or better will be rewarded with a bonus to charm and impress.

children.name: attributes.Spd
children.label: Spd
children.description: Specifically, this is the character's maximum running speed. The Spd. times 20 is the number of yards the character can run in one minute. If the Spd. is 16 or higher, the character has a bonus to all dodge rolls.

options.label: Detect Psionics
options.description: This mental probe will detect psionics in others. There is a 75% chance that the psionic can be detected by other psionics in the area. This will not penetrate or detect a Mind Block, and a Mind Blocked character will not appear to be psionic.

options.label: Animal Control
options.description: Allows a character to command creatures of their own kind. For example, a lion character could control other lions, not tigers or other felines. Two-way communication with the controlled animals is not possible without Animal Speech. The controlled animal will attempt to follow any orders. If the controlled animal is wounded it is allowed an additional saving throw. The psionic can renew Animal Control every 5 minutes, but the animal can roll to save every time.

options.label: Animal Speech
options.description: Allows for complete two-way communication with animals of the same kind as the character. For example, a rat character could use Animal Speech only with other rats, not with mice or other rodents. The animals will recognize the character as one of their own and assume that any human looks are just ugly deformities.

options.label: Bio-Manipulation
options.description: Each of the seven kinds of bio-manipulation must be purchased separately. Each affects only one person per attack and the victim must be within line of vision or their exact location must be known to the psionic. A creature using bio-manipulation can do no other psionics while the effect lasts due to the concentration required. If the psionic is knocked unconscious or uses any other psionic ability, the bio-manipulation immediately stops. The seven types are: Blind (victim is -9 to strike, parry and dodge), Deafness (victim cannot hear and is -6 to parry or dodge attacks from behind, -3 to strike/parry/dodge, and automatically loses initiative), Mute (speech is impossible; victim is -2 to strike, parry and dodge for the first round), Pain (nerve centers are hit directly; victim is -6 to strike/parry/dodge and takes 1 hit point damage per minute), Paralysis (arms and legs stop working; victim cannot parry or dodge), Stun (victim loses one attack per melee, speed is halved, and is -4 to strike/parry/dodge), and Tissue Manipulation (victim feels itchy, cold and hot; -1 to strike, parry and dodge).

options.label: Hypnotic Suggestion
options.description: The psionic may induce their will upon another through the power of suggestion. This is a mental attack that requires eye contact and either verbal or telepathic reinforcement. The suggestion must be a reasonable one; victims will not do things that are completely out of character. For example, the suggestion 'kill yourself' would only work if the victim were already suicidal, while 'you don't see anything unusual' would be very useful for a strange-looking animal character. This works only on intelligent creatures, not on ordinary animals. A victim who saves against Hypnotic Suggestion will be totally unaware of the attack.

options.label: Mind Block
options.description: This mental wall prevents the penetration of Telepathy, Empathy, Empathic Transfer, Detect Psionics and all other psionic attacks. The psionic will not even be aware that a psionic attack is taking place. Note that this also prevents the character from hearing Telepathic Transmission.

options.label: Mind Trap
options.description: A psionic who successfully traps another mind can inflict whatever experience they like onto that mind — for example, making the victim believe they are trapped in a small cell or reliving an episode from the psionic's imagination. One strange effect is that the victim experiences one hour of subjective time for every minute in the trap. Victims can make another saving throw after being trapped; if successful, they realize the experience is imaginary but cannot escape it. Keeping the victim in the trap is simple with a basic environment (cage, desert, ocean), costing the attacker one attack per melee. Complex fantasies require the attacker to lose all other attacks or movements. Victims can optionally be driven temporarily insane if the psionic is insane, the experience is particularly horrible, or the fantasy involves the victim's death; the effect lasts 2-12 hours.

options.label: See Aura
options.description: Allows the psionic to see the alignment, estimated level of power, type of creature (human, mutant, alien, meat-eating animal, grazing animal, etc.) and the physical condition of the creature in their aura. There is a 15% chance per level that the psionic can recognize an aura that has been seen before. Mind Blocked characters are not protected from See Aura.

options.label: Sixth Sense
options.description: A psionic intuition that can protect the character. There will be a premonition of danger when something is immediately threatening, including hidden ambushes, traps, or dangerous situations. Sixth Sense does not indicate what or where the danger is coming from, and does not work to protect anyone traveling with the character. The character must actively specify that the power is being used; it does not work automatically.

options.label: Telepathic Transmission
options.description: Acts as a mind-to-mind speech substitute, highly useful for animals without vocal cords. Range is roughly that of human speech but is not affected by background noise. Does not work with telephones or other communication devices. The psionic can focus the transmission so that only a specific person receives it, or broadcast to several people simultaneously so long as everyone is within the 150-foot range. Non-psionics will assume the Telepathic Transmission is normal speech. Cannot be used to read anything — it is only good for speaking. There is no saving throw because it is not an attack; at worst the psionic can shout unpleasantly and the receiver can choose to ignore the message.
